release: v0.3.0 — Live View window

New feature: Live View — a separate OS window showing all text as
flowing paragraphs with the currently-streaming word highlighted
(theme-aware color + underline) and smooth auto-scroll keeping the
highlight in the top 1/3 of the viewport.

Highlights:
- '+' button in preview panel opens the Live View
- Independent font size slider (18–200)
- Mirrors all playback actions in real time, even when backgrounded
- Opening pauses playback; closing does not affect playback state
- Wayland minimize freeze protection via eglSwapInterval(0) + stale
  child guard
- 13 new unit tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Integrate design improvements to file storage system

Accepted changes from local review:
- Materialized sync journal: sync_changes table with monotonic
  change_seq, replacing opaque cursor strings with concrete integers
- Tombstones for deletions: explicit delete events in the change feed
  prevent offline devices from resurrecting removed files
- status + legal_hold columns on files schema, consistent with §7l
- POST /restore API endpoint (was missing despite trash lifecycle)
- Pending verdict state for enterprise uploads bridging upload flow
  and malware scanning pipeline
- Smart sync materialization states (placeholder/hydrated/pinned)
  in client architecture section
- Finer-grained sync units (OneNote-style page/section sync) as
  conflict resolution strategy
- Real-world notes: OneDrive Files On-Demand, OneNote collaboration-
  aware storage, Evernote USN model and metadata-first sync
- Three new common patterns: metadata-first catch-up, lazy hydration,
  tombstones in change feed
- New references: OneDrive, Evernote docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: disable asar integrity fuse so patched Codex.exe launches correctly

After patching app.asar to inject process.windowsStore = true, the
embedded SHA256 hash in Codex.exe no longer matches. Electron aborts
with FATAL integrity check error.

Use @electron/fuses to flip EnableEmbeddedAsarIntegrityValidation to
OFF in the binary after repacking the asar.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add production-grade sections to file storage design

New sections:
- 7k. Folder move/rename transactions: pointer-based tree with O(1)
  parent_id update, lazy path cache invalidation, cycle detection
- 7l. Trash/restore/legal hold lifecycle: 4-stage deletion pipeline
  (trash → soft delete → hard delete → chunk GC), legal hold schema
  that overrides GC, compliance admin audit trail
- 7m. Selective sync / smart sync: on-demand file download via OS
  virtual filesystem drivers (macOS File Provider, Windows Cloud
  Files API), placeholder files, eviction policy, pinning
- 7n. Malware scanning & abuse controls: async upload scanning
  pipeline, hash-based short-circuit, DLP rules for enterprise,
  shared link re-scan, abuse rate limiting

Enhanced 7i (File Sharing):
- Inherited permission evaluation: eager vs lazy vs hybrid approach
  with cache invalidation on folder permission changes
- Link revocation propagation: CDN purge + API Gateway deny-list +
  short-TTL signed URLs as defense-in-depth
- Enterprise audit trail: append-only audit_events table with async
  Kafka-based writes, admin dashboard queries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Integrate design improvements to chat system

Accepted changes from local review:
- Per-conversation sequencer (seq_no) for strict ordering, replacing
  Snowflake-only ordering which depends on clock sync
- Bucketed partition key (conversation_id, bucket) now in actual schema,
  consistent with hot-partition mitigation in section 8
- Durable idempotency store (message_dedup Cassandra table) as fallback
  when Redis cache is evicted, keyed by (sender_id, device_id, client_msg_id)
- Devices/key bundles table for E2E encryption multi-device support
- sender_key_epoch on conversations + key rotation on membership changes
  (how Signal Sender Keys actually work in practice)
- history_policy (full vs post_join_only) on conversation_members
- Privacy controls: hide last-seen/read-receipts, block-list enforcement
  on presence subscriptions
- Media encryption for E2E chats (client-side encrypt before upload)
- Home-region sequencing model for cross-region consistency
- Multi-device E2E provisioning notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

fix: always repaint Live View so it mirrors every parent action

Previously, child repaints were only sent when sync_live_view_shared
detected a data change (current_index, chunk_size, theme, etc.).
Actions like play/pause that don't immediately change those fields
left the Live View stale until the next stream tick or mouse hover.

Now the parent unconditionally requests a child repaint every frame
while the Live View is open, gated only by the stale-child guard.
egui caches layout internally so redundant repaints are cheap.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: gambtho <14336279+gambtho@users.noreply.github.com>

Add comprehensive file storage & sync design (Dropbox/Drive/OneDrive)

959-line design document covering:
- Capacity estimation (50PB storage, 116K sync ops/sec peak)
- Client sync engine architecture (watcher, chunker, indexer, local DB)
- Chunked resumable upload with dedup check sequence diagram
- Delta sync: content-defined chunking vs fixed-size, binary diff
- Block-level deduplication with SHA-256 content addressing
- Three-tree merge model for conflict resolution
- Long polling notification service (vs WebSocket trade-off)
- Multi-device sync with per-device cursors + offline reconciliation
- File sharing ACL model, versioning with chunk reuse
- Bottlenecks: hot files, metadata DB scaling, large file sync
- Real-world notes: Dropbox Magic Pocket (exabyte-scale blob store,
  12 nines durability, saved $75M/yr vs S3), Nucleus sync engine,
  Edgestore metadata, Google Drive resumable uploads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use dlopen for EGL lookup; restore full background rendering

The previous dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT) approach silently failed because glutin
loads libEGL.so with RTLD_LOCAL, hiding its symbols from the global
table.  Now we dlopen("libEGL.so.1", RTLD_LAZY) to get an explicit
handle, then dlsym on that handle — this correctly finds and calls
eglSwapInterval(0), making swap_buffers non-blocking.

With swap_interval(0) actually working:
- Removed the focus gate from the parent: unfocused-but-visible children
  keep receiving repaints and track the word stream in real time.
- Removed the child early-exit on unfocused: background-visible Live
  View renders full content, not an empty panel.
- Stale-child guard (1 s) remains as a safety net for compositors that
  don't honor swap interval 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix connparse handling for scheme-less `//...` WSH shorthand URIs (#3006)

`pkg/remote/connparse` was failing on shorthand WSH inputs that omit the
`wsh://` scheme, including remote hosts, WSL targets, and Windows local
paths. The parser was splitting on `://` too early and misclassifying
leading `//` inputs before WSH shorthand handling ran.

- **What changed**
- Detect scheme-less WSH shorthand up front with `strings.HasPrefix(uri,
"//")`
- Route those inputs through the existing WSH path parsing flow instead
of the generic `://` split path
- Reuse the same shorthand flag when deciding whether to parse as
remote/local WSH vs current-path shorthand

- **Behavioral impact**
- `//conn/path/to/file` now parses as host `conn` with path
`path/to/file`
- `//wsl://Ubuntu/path/to/file` now preserves the WSL host and absolute
path shape
- `//local/C:\path\to\file` now parses as local Windows shorthand
instead of being treated as a current-path string

- **Scope**
  - Keeps the existing test expectations intact
  - Limits the change to `pkg/remote/connparse/connparse.go`

```go
isWshShorthand := strings.HasPrefix(uri, "//")

if isWshShorthand {
    rest = strings.TrimPrefix(uri, "//")
} else if len(split) > 1 {
    scheme = split[0]
    rest = strings.TrimPrefix(split[1], "//")
}
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions,
customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context
Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent
tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Release Notes entry to the settings menu (#3005)

This adds a **Release Notes** action to `SettingsFloatingWindow` in the
requested position: after **Secrets** and before **Help**. It also wires
that action to open the existing `onboarding-upgrade-patch.tsx` UI as a
standalone modal, so release notes remain accessible even when automatic
upgrade onboarding would not render for up-to-date clients.

- **Settings menu**
- Adds a new **Release Notes** item to
`frontend/app/workspace/widgets.tsx`
  - Places it between **Secrets** and **Help**
- Uses the existing modal system rather than creating a new view/block
path

- **Release notes launch path**
  - Registers `UpgradeOnboardingPatch` in the modal registry
- Opens it from the settings menu via
`modalsModel.pushModal("UpgradeOnboardingPatch", { isReleaseNotes: true
})`

- **Standalone modal behavior**
- Extends `UpgradeOnboardingPatch` with a lightweight `isReleaseNotes`
mode
- In release-notes mode, closing the modal pops the stacked modal
instead of toggling `upgradeOnboardingOpen`
- Preserves the existing automatic upgrade-onboarding flow and version
metadata update behavior for the original path

```tsx
{
    icon: "book-open",
    label: "Release Notes",
    onClick: () => {
        modalsModel.pushModal("UpgradeOnboardingPatch", { isReleaseNotes: true });
        onClose();
    },
}
```

- **<screenshot>**
  - Release notes modal content:
![Release Notes
modal](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/914041a0-1248-4d1a-8eed-125713f7b067)

commit.message:
[ts-http-runtime] Fix URL construction in urlHelpers.ts (#37302)

There are cases where the `routePath` isn't a real path, but in the form
of `"?restype=service&comp=properties"`. They are supposed to be query
parameters but today are appended to the endpoint directly. This caused
issues when the endpoint already contains query parameters (e.g.,
storage SAS URL).

This PR handles this case and ports code from `@azure/core-client` for appending path and merging query params.


### Packages impacted by this PR
`@typespec/ts-http-runtime`

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add shift ops, fix macros, improve _byte_ge_const_flag n=128

Macro system:
- Fix $param substitution in parameterized macros: ADD(x,y) with
  'set $a + $b on a' now correctly expands to 'set $x + $y on x'
- Add #ifdef / #ifndef / #else / #endif conditional compilation
- Add #if 1 / #if 0 for simple conditional blocks
- Add #elif for chained conditionals
- Add backslash line continuation for multi-line macro definitions
- Stack-based tracking ensures nested conditionals work correctly

Shift operators (<< and >>):
- Tokenizer now recognizes '<<' and '>>' as two-character tokens
- 'set $x << N on y' and 'set $x >> N on y' compile for byte/int types
- Right operand must be a compile-time integer constant
- Emits N successive calls to _shift_left/right_multi_byte

_byte_ge_const_flag n=128 performance:
- Add _extract_bit7(pos, out_flag): uses 7 applications of _divmod2_cell
  to extract MSB in O(255) BF steps vs O(16384) for _compare_bytes_unsigned
- _byte_ge_const_flag(pos, 128, flag) now routes through _extract_bit7
- This speeds up _shift_left_multi_byte at BF runtime (64x for n=128 case)
  making left-shifts and multiplication much faster when executed

Tests:
- New tests/test_macros.py: 37 tests covering tokenizer, conditionals,
  line continuation, dollar-param macros, shift operators, and integration
- Total test count: 111 passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

implement GameRoom GenServer (CAR-35) (#85)

* implement GameRoom GenServer (CAR-35)

Adds Carddo.GameRoom GenServer to coordinate live game state, call the
Rust NIF for move resolution, and broadcast updates to channel subscribers.
Registers processes via Carddo.GameRegistry (Registry, unique keys).

* add opencode action and update gitignore

* add instructions for opencode and copilot

* Fix GameRoom robustness issues from code review

- Fix NIF 3-tuple return value handling (was causing CaseClauseError)
- Add explicit 30s timeout to GenServer.call for NIF operations
- Replace Jason.decode! with safe decode + error handling
- Replace broadcast! with broadcast to avoid crashing on PubSub failures
- Change Task.async to Task.start for fire-and-forget checkpointing
- Remove overly-broad handle_info clauses that could swallow messages
- Add comprehensive test suite (10 tests)

- Add broadcast verification test using PubSub.subscribe
- Add test for invalid start_link options

104 tests pass.

- Add guard clause to start_link/1 for non-map input
- Change TODO log from info to debug level

105 tests pass.

Update aiusechat read_dir tests for typed entry output (#3007)

`pkg/aiusechat/tools_readdir_test.go` was still asserting the old
`entries` payload shape after `read_dir` moved to returning typed
directory entries. This caused the `pkg/aiusechat` test failures even
though the tool behavior itself was already correct.

- **Align test expectations with current callback output**
- Update `TestReadDirCallback` to treat `entries` as
`[]fileutil.DirEntryOut`
- Assert directory/file classification via the `Dir` field instead of
map lookups

- **Fix truncation/sorting coverage**
- Update `TestReadDirSortBeforeTruncate` to validate the typed slice
returned by `readDirCallback`
- Preserve the existing intent of the test: directories should still be
sorted ahead of files before truncation

- **Keep scope limited to stale tests**
  - No changes to `read_dir` implementation or output contract
  - Only the broken test assumptions were corrected

```go
entries, ok := resultMap["entries"].([]fileutil.DirEntryOut)
if !ok {
    t.Fatalf("entries is not a slice of DirEntryOut")
}

for _, entry := range entries {
    if entry.Dir {
        // directory assertions
    }
}
```

commit.message:
fix: gate repaints on child focus to prevent Wayland minimize freeze

Three layers of defense against the minimize freeze:

1. eglSwapInterval(0) — makes swap_buffers non-blocking (primary)
2. Parent focus gate — skips request_repaint_of when child_focused is
   Some(false), preventing new repaints from being queued
3. Child early-exit — when unfocused, renders an empty panel and
   returns immediately, catching stray repaints already in the queue

When the Live View is unfocused but visible (user watching the main
stream), OS events (mouse hover/enter) still trigger child renders
that pick up the latest shared state on demand.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add global package registry with use(), register(), and artifact caching

Introduces PackageRegistry.jl — a unified module that merges Scaffold.jl,
coordinates DependencyResolver and EnvironmentDoctor, and provides:

- use(name): one-call wrapper loading (registry → deps → env → build → wrap → load)
- register(toml): SHA256 content-addressed TOML storage in ~/.replibuild/registry/
- list_registry() / unregister(): registry management
- Global build artifact caching in ~/.replibuild/builds/<hash>/
- Environment check caching with 24h TTL in ~/.replibuild/toolchain.toml
- discover() auto-registers on success
- scaffold_package() pulls from registry for known targets
- REPLIBUILD_HOME env var for custom registry location

All 28 tests pass + integration suite (stdlib, vtable, callback, benchmark).

commit.message:
fix: remove focus gate so Live View updates while unfocused

The parent-side focus guard (child_focused != Some(false)) blocked ALL
repaints when the child was unfocused — including the normal case where
the user watches the main stream with the Live View visible beside it.

With eglSwapInterval(0) now making swap_buffers non-blocking, the focus
gate is unnecessary.  The stale-child guard (1 s timeout) remains as a
safety net for compositors that don't honor swap interval 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ci] Add merge queue support to CI workflow

Add merge_group event trigger so the CI pipeline runs when PRs enter the
merge queue targeting dev. Skip the branch-up-to-date precheck for merge
queue events (the queue already ensures currency) and update all job-level
if-conditions to bypass precheck/draft gates for merge_group runs. Release
and publish jobs are unaffected since they already require refs/heads/dev.

commit.message:
fix: use dlsym for EGL symbols instead of link-time binding

Replace the unsafe extern "C" declarations (which required -lEGL at
link time) with dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT, ...) lookups at runtime.  glutin
has already loaded libEGL.so by the time the child viewport renders,
so the symbols are present in the process.  This avoids requiring
libegl-dev at build time and keeps the binary free of additional
link-time dependencies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: separate auth tokens for Copilot and Git Backup

Introduce independent environment variables and config fields to decouple
Copilot CLI authentication from Git Backup authentication:

- COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN / agent.copilotGithubToken for dedicated Copilot token
- GIT_BACKUP_AUTH_USER / gitBackup.authUser for Git Backup HTTPS username
- GIT_BACKUP_AUTH_PASSWORD / gitBackup.authPassword for Git Backup HTTPS password

Both tokens are passed separately to the Copilot CLI subprocess, letting
it handle its own env var priority. Git Backup now supports configurable
HTTPS auth with fallback chain (authUser → authorEmail → x-access-token,
authPassword → GITHUB_TOKEN env → empty).

Full backward compatibility maintained: existing GITHUB_TOKEN-only setups
continue to work without changes.

Resolves #248

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Chen <Jim@ChenJ.im>
